description: CSV file reading and writing. Implements classes to read and write tabular data in CSV format, with support for Excel-generated CSV files.

functions.name: reader

params.name: csvfile
params.description: An iterable object that returns strings for each row, typically a file object opened for reading

params.name: dialect
params.description: Name of a registered dialect or a Dialect subclass (default 'excel')

params.name: delimiter
params.description: A one-character string used to separate fields (default ',')

params.name: quotechar
params.description: A one-character string used to quote fields containing special characters (default '"')

params.name: quoting
params.description: Controls when quotes are generated by the writer or recognized by the reader (e.g., csv.QUOTE_MINIMAL)

returns.description: A reader object that iterates over lines in the given csvfile, returning each row as a list of strings

functions.description: Return a reader object which will iterate over lines in the given csvfile. Each row is returned as a list of strings.

functions.name: writer

params.name: csvfile
params.description: A file object with a write() method, opened for writing

params.name: dialect
params.description: Name of a registered dialect or a Dialect subclass (default 'excel')

params.name: delimiter
params.description: A one-character string used to separate fields (default ',')

params.name: quotechar
params.description: A one-character string used to quote fields containing special characters (default '"')

params.name: quoting
params.description: Controls when quotes are generated (e.g., csv.QUOTE_MINIMAL)

returns.description: A writer object with writerow() and writerows() methods

functions.description: Return a writer object responsible for converting the user's data into delimited strings on the given file-like object.

functions.name: register_dialect

params.name: name
params.description: The name to register the dialect under

params.name: dialect
params.description: A Dialect subclass (optional if keyword formatting parameters are supplied)

params.name: fmtparams
params.description: Keyword formatting parameters (delimiter, quotechar, etc.)

functions.description: Associate a dialect name with the given dialect class or formatting parameters. The name can then be used as the dialect argument to reader or writer.

functions.name: unregister_dialect

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the dialect to remove

functions.description: Delete the dialect associated with name from the dialect registry. Raises an Error if name is not a registered dialect name.

functions.name: get_dialect

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the dialect to retrieve

returns.description: The Dialect instance associated with the given name

functions.description: Return the dialect associated with the given name. Raises an Error if the name is not a registered dialect.

functions.name: list_dialects
functions.description: Return the names of all registered dialects.

functions.name: field_size_limit

params.name: new_limit
params.description: If given, sets the new maximum field size. If omitted, the current limit is returned without changing it.

functions.description: Returns the current maximum field size allowed by the parser. If new_limit is given, this becomes the new limit.

classes.name: DictReader
classes.description: Create an object which operates like a regular reader but maps the information read into a dict whose keys are given by the fieldnames parameter or inferred from the first row.

params.name: csvfile
params.description: An iterable object that returns strings for each row

params.name: fieldnames
params.description: List of keys for the dict. If omitted, values in the first row of the CSV file are used as field names.

params.name: restkey
params.description: Key to use for values in rows that have more fields than fieldnames (default None)

params.name: restval
params.description: Value to use for keys in rows that have fewer fields than fieldnames (default None)

params.name: dialect
params.description: Name of a registered dialect (default 'excel')

constructors.description: Create a new DictReader. If fieldnames is omitted, the first row of the CSV file is used as the field names.

methods.name: next

returns.description: Dictionary mapping fieldnames to values for the current row

methods.description: Return the next row of the reader's iterable object as a dict, parsed according to the current dialect.

fields.name: fieldnames
fields.description: List of field names used as dictionary keys. If not passed to the constructor, identified from the first row of the CSV file.

fields.name: restkey
fields.description: Key to use when a row has more fields than fieldnames

fields.name: restval
fields.description: Default value for keys when a row has fewer fields than fieldnames

fields.name: dialect
fields.description: The dialect in use by the reader

fields.name: line_num
fields.description: The number of lines read from the source iterator

classes.name: DictWriter
classes.description: Create an object which operates like a regular writer but maps dictionaries onto output rows. The fieldnames parameter identifies the order in which values are written to the CSV file.

params.name: csvfile
params.description: A file object with a write() method

params.name: fieldnames
params.description: Sequence of keys identifying the order in which values are written

params.name: restval
params.description: Value to write if a key from fieldnames is missing from the dict (default '')

params.name: extrasaction
params.description: Action if a dict has keys not in fieldnames: 'raise' (default) raises ValueError, 'ignore' ignores extra keys

params.name: dialect
params.description: Name of a registered dialect (default 'excel')

methods.name: writerow

params.name: rowdict
params.description: Dictionary mapping fieldnames to values

methods.description: Write a row with the given dictionary, using the fieldnames to determine the order.

methods.name: writerows

params.name: rowdicts
params.description: List of dictionaries to write as rows

methods.description: Write all the given dictionaries as rows.

methods.name: writeheader
methods.description: Write a row with the field names (as specified in the constructor). Available in Python 2.7+.

fields.name: fieldnames
fields.description: Sequence of keys identifying the order in which values are written

classes.name: Dialect
classes.description: The Dialect class is a container for the parameters used to define the format of a CSV file. Subclass this to create custom CSV formats.

fields.name: delimiter
fields.description: A one-character string used to separate fields. Default is ','.

fields.name: quotechar
fields.description: A one-character string used to quote fields containing special characters, such as the delimiter or quotechar, or which contain newline characters. Default is '"'.

fields.name: escapechar
fields.description: A one-character string used to escape the delimiter if quoting is set to QUOTE_NONE, and the quotechar if doublequote is False. Default is None.

fields.name: doublequote
fields.description: Controls how instances of quotechar appearing inside a field are quoted. When True, the character is doubled. When False, the escapechar is used. Default is True.

fields.name: skipinitialspace
fields.description: When True, whitespace immediately following the delimiter is ignored. Default is False.

fields.name: lineterminator
fields.description: The string used to terminate lines produced by the writer. Default is '\r\n'.

fields.name: quoting
fields.description: Controls when quotes should be generated by the writer and recognized by the reader. Default is QUOTE_MINIMAL.

classes.name: Sniffer
classes.description: Analyzes a sample of CSV text and returns a Dialect subclass reflecting the parameters found. Also provides a method to determine if a file has a header row.

methods.name: sniff

params.name: sample
params.description: A sample of the CSV text to analyze

params.name: delimiters
params.description: String of possible delimiter characters. If omitted, all common delimiters are tried.

returns.description: A Dialect subclass reflecting the detected format parameters

methods.description: Analyze the given sample and return a Dialect subclass reflecting the parameters found. If the optional delimiters parameter is given, it is interpreted as a string containing possible valid delimiter characters.

methods.name: has_header

params.name: sample
params.description: A sample of the CSV text to analyze

returns.description: True if the first row appears to be a header row

methods.description: Analyze the sample text (presumed to be in CSV format) and return True if the first row appears to be a series of column headers.

classes.name: Error
classes.description: Exception raised by the csv module for any errors detected during CSV processing (e.g., malformed data, field size exceeded).

classes.name: excel
classes.description: The excel class defines the usual properties of an Excel-generated CSV file. It is registered as the dialect name 'excel'.

classes.name: excel_tab
classes.description: The excel_tab class defines the usual properties of an Excel-generated TAB-delimited file. It is registered as the dialect name 'excel-tab'.

constants.name: QUOTE_ALL
constants.description: Instructs writer objects to quote all fields.

constants.name: QUOTE_MINIMAL
constants.description: Instructs writer objects to only quote those fields which contain special characters such as the delimiter, quotechar, or any of the characters in lineterminator.

constants.name: QUOTE_NONNUMERIC
constants.description: Instructs writer objects to quote all non-numeric fields. Instructs the reader to convert all non-quoted fields to type float.

constants.name: QUOTE_NONE
constants.description: Instructs writer objects to never quote fields. The current escapechar is used to escape the delimiter if needed. Instructs reader to perform no special processing of quote characters.
